title: Man page validation: part 2 of 2
body:
This is the script that runs 'skopeo COMMAND --help' and
cross-checks that all the option flags are documented
in man pages, and vice-versa (all options listed in man
pages appear in COMMAND's --help message).

Copied from podman, with changes for skopeo-land (removing
the rst checks, and conforming to skopeo conventions).

Signed-off-by: Ed Santiago <santiago@redhat.com>

comments.body: I think this is going to fail, because I bet Macs don't have perl, but I'll figure that all out tomorrow when I incorporate @mtrmac's review feedback from #1347. Right now I just want to see how far we get. (It passes on my home system).

FWIW
```
% perl --version

This is perl 5, version 30, subversion 2 (v5.30.2) … 
```
on macOS 11.4, but it’s deprecated (Google quickly finds https://forums.macrumors.com/threads/are-the-scripting-languages-still-here.2300956/ ).

But with that I’m done for today.

FWIW I have filed https://github.com/containers/skopeo/issues/1354 for the “/dev/tcp/127.0.0.1/5000: No route to host” failures. These come up about daily now, and a re-start almost(?) always succeeds.

(So far, I’m ashamed to admit, I’ve just been pressing Re-run because I don’t know where to start with “no route to 127.0.0.1”.)

OK, I think this is ready for review. I've reworked the CI invocation so it doesn't run on macs; incorporated @mtrmac's feedback from #1347 (the few bits he didn't fix in #1351); and removed unnecessary bits from the xref script.
@lsm5 not really, this is just the stupid github bug that complains if PR isn't on the very tip of main. The warning can (and should) be ignored.

> @lsm5 not really, this is just the stupid github bug that complains if PR isn't on the very tip of main. The warning can (and should) be ignored.

ah i remember now. my bad.

It failed. Moving on to other things, this isn't something I can debug. If someone would like to take over this PR, I'm fine with it. 
```
"${GOSRC}/${SCRIPT_BASE}/runner.sh" setup
WARNING: It does not appear that containers/automation was installed.
         Functionality of most of /var/tmp/go/src/github.com/containers/skopeo/./contrib/cirrus/runner.sh will be negatively
         impacted.
/var/tmp/go/src/github.com/containers/skopeo/./contrib/cirrus/runner.sh: line 87: req_env_vars: command not found

Exit status: 127
```
I don’t know that much about the CI setup, but comparing with `validate_task`: just replacing the three `script:` entries with a simple `make validate-docs`, with no `runner.sh` use, might work.

> If someone would like to take over this PR, I'm fine with it.

oh, I know how to fix that.  I can take this over, np.
